Perspective and angle of a 3D drawing figure
You can apply perspective to a 3D drawing figure for a sense of distance, as well as change the camera angle. Learn how to use these features for more dynamic poses.

Once you have decided on the composition you want to draw, confirm with “OK”. Try out various compositions using the 3D drawing figure.

You can freely adjust the pose, body shape, and head-to-body ratio of a 3D drawing figure. Select a pose or body shape that you want to draw.

Add flat colors with the Fill tool
The Fill tool allows you to fill closed areas of line art with one tap. Select the tool and try it out.
When you select the Fill tool, the fill launcher will appear. Here, you can change the fill method and settings.
There are two ways to fill. You can switch between filling methods from the launcher.
You can paint a specific area by tapping, and paint a continuous area by swiping. Let's try painting some clothes.
You can lasso an area to paint only the enclosed areas inside. Try drawing a circle around the hat to fill it.
You can change the detailed settings from the button on the far right. There are even more functions that make coloring easier, so make sure to check them out.

Switch to Studio Mode with button
Switch to Studio Mode from the menu
You can move, rotate, and transform an object using the handles. If there is a selection, only the content within the selection will be targeted.
You can select an area by freehand or by drawing a shape. After selecting, you can only draw within the specified area.
You can pick up the color from the place where you tap the canvas to change the drawing color.
Color filters can adjust the balance of color and brightness. Effects such as blurring and line extraction can be used to give special effects to your drawing.
You can adjust the selection brush's “>” button to adjust the stabilization. To rearrange brushes, hold and drag a brush and release it at the desired position.
You can save what you've drawn by tapping the save icon. Make sure to save your drawing frequently so you can continue drawing whenever you want.
You can share your artwork with “Export image” and “Export timelapse”.
You can search for and add new brushes by pressing the + button and tapping “Add recommended brushes”.
You can adjust the transparency (opacity) of each brush. When the opacity value is lower, the brush will be less opaque and more transparent.
Tap “Canvas setting” to change the size of the drawing area without changing the size of the content on the canvas. To resize the canvas and the content at the same time, use “Resize all”.
You can use your painting brush like an eraser to remove parts of your drawing while keeping the same brush texture.
Some brushes and gradient tools use both the main and sub colors.
After drawing a shape, hold the stylus in place for a moment to automatically convert your freehand shape to straight lines or clean shapes.
Tap the menu command to switch to Studio Mode. You can continue drawing on your current canvas.
Check out the tutorial to learn about basic features and useful tools.

Do you want to rasterize the layer?
You need to rasterize the layer in order to move or transform it.
Do you want to convert the layer to color?
To apply the filter, the expression color of the selected layer must be converted to color.
Do you want to extract lines?
You can extract lines drawn on paper, etc. The parts outside the lines will become transparent.
Select an area to apply a filter
Learn how to add and rearrange layers. Using layers effectively can save you time and improve quality.
Tap the three dots to add images with the menu
Try adding an image to use as a background.
Move the layer with your new image so that is below "Window".
You can move all the layers in a folder at once. Try dragging the "Background" folder below the "Character" layer.

The order of layers is especially important when using functions such as blending modes. By separating elements into different layers and organizing the layer palette, you can have greater control over your artwork.
This layer type is not supported in Simple Mode, so some features are unavailable.

Swipe to the side to extend the layer palette. This allows you to always see the layer details.
